title: DreamOutput
description: Output of a weekly dream job: an offline-cognition run that consolidates memory, clusters failures, simulates adversarial inputs, and proposes promotion candidates (memory updates, generated tests, skill patches, backlog items). Every candidate carries evidence and a human_review_required flag.

id.description: Dream run identifier. Format: dream-<YYYY>-W<WW> where WW is the ISO week number. Example: dream-2026-W21.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the dream job finished.

generated_by.description: Identifier of the agent or pipeline that produced this output. Example: dream-job@athena-site or claude-opus-4-7.

scope.description: What inputs the dream run consumed.

repos.description: Repositories included in the analysis window.

lookback_days.description: How many days of history were scanned.

sources.description: Input streams consumed. Common values: runs, traces, postmortems, decisions, eval_reports, screenshots.

modes.description: Which offline-cognition modes ran. At least one is required.

report_path.description: Path (relative to the owning repo root) to the human-readable narrative report this dream run produced.

candidates.description: Promotion candidates this run proposes. Each candidate is one of the four typed shapes below and must carry evidence plus an explicit human_review_required flag. Discriminator is target_kind; earlier emissions used kind and product-repo validators tolerate both during the transition.

evidence_array.description: Pointers to the artifacts, runs, or traces that justify the candidate.

memory_update.$comment: Canonical discriminator is target_kind. Earlier emissions used kind; validators in product repos tolerate both during the transition. Future emissions should use target_kind.

target_kind.description: Discriminator. Canonical field is target_kind; legacy emissions used kind (validators tolerate both).

target.description: Path of the memory file the update would land in.

human_review_required.description: Whether a human must approve before this memory update lands. Default true.

test_generation.$comment: Canonical discriminator is target_kind. Earlier emissions used kind; validators in product repos tolerate both during the transition. Future emissions should use target_kind.

target_kind.description: Discriminator. Canonical field is target_kind; legacy emissions used kind (validators tolerate both).

test_path.description: Path of the proposed test file.

human_review_required.description: Whether a human must review before the test is added to CI. Default true.

skill_patch.$comment: Canonical discriminator is target_kind. Earlier emissions used kind; validators in product repos tolerate both during the transition. Future emissions should use target_kind.

target_kind.description: Discriminator. Canonical field is target_kind; legacy emissions used kind (validators tolerate both).

skill_id.description: Id of the skill being patched.

patch.description: Proposed change to instructions, scripts, or eval set.

human_review_required.description: Whether a human must approve the patch. Default true.

backlog_item.$comment: Canonical discriminator is target_kind. Earlier emissions used kind; validators in product repos tolerate both during the transition. Future emissions should use target_kind.

target_kind.description: Discriminator. Canonical field is target_kind; legacy emissions used kind (validators tolerate both).

title.description: Short title for the proposed work.

rationale.description: Why the work matters, grounded in the evidence.

human_review_required.description: Whether a human must triage before this becomes a tracked item. Default true.
